title: Connections, Channels, Heartbeats, Errors, and Recovery · Inference Brief: Channel Ownership
summary: A service opens one TCP connection per message, shares one channel across unrelated concurrent owners, disables heartbeats, retries instantly, and redeclares consumers before topology or credentials recover. Planned artifact: Add and verify the channel ownership increment in connection, channel, heartbeat, and recovery state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.
Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Channel Ownership · Theory Channel Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1724 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound heartbeat and reconnect policy while rejecting stale channel-generation callbacks; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Channel Ownership contract for the supplied data.
